title: Chronic sinus symptom follow-up

examination.text: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.
examination.rationale: The exact chronic-sinusitis section supports clinician-performed ENT assessment, reviewed imaging or specialist reports, modifying-factor context, and polyp documentation without interpreting CT or ordering endoscopy.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 7A–10 — chronic distinction, objective confirmation, modifiers, testing, and polyps

treatment_and_management.text: Supports interval change, persistent or worsening symptom review, clinician reassessment, and documented follow-up without changing treatment.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact reassessment statement supports interval change, symptom impact, associated or negative features, concerns, and clinician reassessment without declaring treatment failure.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports interval change, persistent or worsening symptom review, clinician reassessment, and documented follow-up without changing treatment.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update

exact_section.heading: Key action statement 6 — treatment-failure or worsening reassessment

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact symptom-pattern section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions only when documented; it does not label bacterial disease or choose an antibiotic.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 1A–1B — acute rhinosinusitis distinction and clinical diagnosis context

sources_used.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
sources_used.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation

source_limitations: Exact duration, symptom pattern, smell, obstruction, discharge, facial pressure, dental cause, asthma, immune status, examination, endoscopy, imaging, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
